prompt:
Passage 1
A regional repair company recently updated its process for assigning emergency service calls. Previously, dispatchers contacted technicians by phone one at a time until someone was available. During busy periods, this process could delay the response to urgent jobs.
The company now uses a shared dispatch platform. When an emergency call is entered, available technicians receive a notification with the customer location, problem description, and priority level. The first qualified technician who accepts the job is assigned automatically.
Managers report that the new process has reduced response time for urgent calls. Dispatchers still monitor assignments, but they spend less time making repeated phone calls and more time assisting customers.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Dispatchers no longer assist customers.
choices.B: A shared dispatch platform improved emergency service-call assignment.
choices.C: Technicians now choose only non-urgent jobs.
choices.D: The company stopped accepting emergency calls.

explanation: The passage explains how the shared dispatch platform improved emergency service-call assignment by notifying available technicians and reducing response time.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning November 6, employees must inspect portable ladders before each use. The inspection should include checking steps, rails, feet, labels, and locking parts. Any ladder that appears damaged should be removed from service and reported to a supervisor.
The updated requirement was created after several ladders were found with worn feet or missing safety labels. The purpose is to identify problems before a ladder is used, not after an incident occurs.
Supervisors will review proper ladder inspection during safety meetings this month.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To announce a new ladder inspection requirement
choices.B: To remove all ladders from the workplace
choices.C: To explain a new payroll system

explanation: The notice announces the requirement to inspect portable ladders before each use.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many organizations use meeting agendas to make discussions more organized. An agenda lists the topics to be discussed, the person responsible for each topic, and sometimes the amount of time planned for each section.
A clear agenda helps participants prepare before a meeting begins. If employees know that a staffing issue, equipment purchase, or safety concern will be discussed, they can bring the correct records or questions. Agendas also help meetings stay focused by reminding participants of the main purpose.
However, an agenda is useful only when it is followed with some discipline. If participants spend too much time on unrelated issues, the meeting may end before important topics are addressed.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Meeting agendas can help organize discussions when they are followed.
choices.B: Meetings should never include planned topics.
choices.C: Agendas are useful only after meetings are over.
choices.D: Employees should avoid preparing for meetings.

explanation: The passage explains that agendas make meetings more organized and useful when participants follow them.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before barcode scanning became common in grocery stores, cashiers often entered prices manually or used price stickers on individual items. This process could be slow, especially when labels were missing or difficult to read.
Barcode scanning changed checkout by allowing cashiers to scan a code that identified the item and price in the store system. This made checkout faster and reduced some pricing errors. It also helped stores collect information about sales patterns and inventory movement.
Today, barcode scanning is used in many industries beyond grocery stores, including shipping, healthcare, libraries, and manufacturing. The basic purpose remains the same: to identify items quickly and accurately.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Barcode scanning improved item identification and checkout efficiency.
choices.B: Grocery stores no longer use cashiers.
choices.C: Price stickers are always faster than scanning.
choices.D: Barcodes are used only in libraries.

explanation: The passage focuses on barcode scanning as a technology that improved item identification and checkout speed.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional transit agency recently updated its bus shelter maintenance program. Previously, shelters were inspected only after riders reported broken glass, damaged benches, or missing schedule signs. This sometimes meant problems remained for several days before repair crews were notified.
The new program places shelters on a rotating inspection schedule. Crews check each shelter for damage, cleanliness, lighting problems, and schedule information. Reports are entered into a maintenance system, where supervisors assign repair tasks based on urgency.
Transit officials say the program has improved the appearance and usability of shelters. Riders have also reported fewer complaints about missing information at stops.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Bus shelters are no longer used by riders.
choices.B: A rotating inspection program improved bus shelter maintenance.
choices.C: Riders now repair broken shelters themselves.
choices.D: Transit agencies stopped posting schedule information.

explanation: The passage explains that rotating inspections improved bus shelter maintenance and reduced complaints.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many workplaces use lockout procedures when equipment must be serviced or repaired. These procedures are intended to prevent equipment from starting unexpectedly while someone is working on it.
A lockout procedure may require employees to shut down the equipment, disconnect power sources, apply locks or tags, and verify that the equipment cannot operate. Workers must follow the required steps before beginning maintenance.
Training is important because skipping a step can create serious hazards. Employees also need to understand who is authorized to apply or remove locks and what to do if a lockout problem occurs.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Lockout procedures help protect workers during equipment service or repair.
choices.B: Equipment should be repaired while running.
choices.C: Locks are used only to protect office doors.
choices.D: Training is unnecessary for maintenance work.

explanation: The passage explains that lockout procedures protect workers by preventing equipment from starting unexpectedly during service.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many public parks use trail markers to help visitors navigate safely. Markers may show trail names, distances, difficulty levels, directions, or warnings about closed sections.
Trail markers are especially useful when paths cross or when visitors are unfamiliar with the area. Clear markers can reduce wrong turns and help visitors estimate how much time or effort a route may require. In larger parks, markers may also assist emergency responders in locating someone who needs help.
Park staff must maintain markers because signs can fade, fall, or become blocked by vegetation. A missing or unreadable marker can create confusion, especially near intersections.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Trail markers help park visitors navigate and must be maintained.
choices.B: Public parks should remove all signs.
choices.C: Visitors should avoid unfamiliar trails.
choices.D: Trail difficulty levels are never useful.

explanation: The passage explains that trail markers guide visitors and must be maintained so they remain clear and useful.

prompt:
Passage 8
An apprenticeship preparation class recently added short “error review” sessions after practice quizzes. Instead of simply marking answers right or wrong, instructors ask students to identify why an incorrect answer was chosen.
Students may discover that they misread the question, skipped a key word, rushed through arithmetic, or chose an answer that seemed familiar but did not match the passage. The goal is to help students recognize patterns in their mistakes.
Instructors explain that error review is not meant to embarrass students. It is a way to turn mistakes into useful study information. Several students have reported that reviewing errors helps them avoid repeating the same mistake on later quizzes.

Why did the class add error review sessions?

choices.A: To help students understand why they chose incorrect answers
choices.B: To remove practice quizzes from the course
choices.C: To make students avoid reviewing mistakes
choices.D: To replace all instruction with grading

explanation: The class added error review so students can understand why they chose incorrect answers.
